MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHINA BASIN BALLPARK COMPANY, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JOSEPH J. POPPEN, ESQUIRE (MEIS & ASSOCIATES, 415.981.4612) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. STEPHEN J. SCHORI, ESQUIRE (JOSEPH COSTELLA & ASSOCIATES, 925.945.4491) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT CHINA BASIN BALLPARK COMPANY.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. The motion of China Basin as to the negligence claim is denied. The Brooks' declaration creates a triable issue of fact within the meaning of the decision in Branco v. Kearny Moto Park, Inc. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 184. The motion is granted as to the products liability claim because plaintiff has failed to raise a triable issue of fact that this defendant was a "link in the chain". San Diego Hospital Assn. v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 8, 15. MR. POPPEN TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE COCA-COLA COMPANY AND DELPHI PRODUCTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JOSEPH J. POPPEN, ESQUIRE (MEIS & ASSOCIATES, 415.981.4612) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. STEPHEN W. OWENS, ESQUIRE (NOLEN & OWENS, 916.733.0600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS COCA COLA AND DELPHI.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. The moving parties have shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's discovery responses. The motion of Delphi and Coca-Cola is denied because the declaration of Brooks creates a triable issue of fact as to whether the slide was defectively designed in such a manner as to create an extreme risk of danger of the type that caused plaintiff's injury.
